# SourceryKit

> Python SDK for Provably: verifiable guardrails for AI agents. Intercepts an
> agent's outbound HTTP calls, enforces endpoint allow-lists, and checks the
> agent's claimed output against the raw data actually fetched. Hallucinated or
> tampered values are CAUGHT, not shipped. Async; supports httpx, aiohttp, requests.

## Start

- [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md) — agent entry point: the flow, rules, and links

## Docs

- [Onboarding & setup (one-time, needs a human)](docs/onboarding.md)
- [End-to-end walkthrough](docs/example.md)
- [API reference](docs/src/api.md)
- [CLI reference](docs/cli.md)
- [Architecture](docs/architecture.md)
- [Intercepting HTTP calls](docs/intercept.md)
- [Handoff payload & verdicts](docs/handoff.md)
- [Trusted endpoints](docs/trusted-endpoints.md)

## Cookbooks (runnable)

- [OpenAI Agents SDK](cookbooks/openai_agents/) — structured output via `output_type`
- [Claude Agent SDK](cookbooks/claude_agent/) — structured output via `output_format` json_schema
- [LangChain](cookbooks/langchain_agent/) — structured output via `response_format`

## Install

`pip install sourcerykit` (Python 3.12+, PyPI: sourcerykit)
